# 'gs branch submit --regenerate-message' uses the configured branch
# message updater to regenerate the PR title and body on re-submit.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-04-05T16:40:32Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'

# set up a fake GitHub remote
shamhub init
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
shamhub register alice
git push origin main

env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# create and submit a branch
git add feature.txt
gs bc -m 'Add feature' feature1
gs branch submit --fill
stderr 'Created #'

# configure the message updater
chmod 755 $WORK/gen.sh
git config spice.message.generator $WORK/gen.sh

# make a change and re-submit
cp $WORK/extra/feature-update.txt feature.txt
git add feature.txt
git commit -m 'update feature'

gs branch submit --regenerate-message
stderr 'Updated #'

shamhub dump changes
cmpenvJSON stdout $WORK/golden/changes.json

-- repo/feature.txt --
Contents of feature

-- extra/feature-update.txt --
New contents of feature

-- golden/changes.json --
[
  {
    "number": 1,
    "state": "open",
    "title": "Updated Title",
    "body": "Updated body text",
    "html_url": "$SHAMHUB_URL/alice/example/change/1",
    "head": {
      "repository": {
        "owner": "alice",
        "name": "example"
      },
      "ref": "feature1",
      "sha": "f4ab6ac87db56e8f0d7f2e6199450716884ee7b8"
    },
    "base": {
      "repository": {
        "owner": "alice",
        "name": "example"
      },
      "ref": "main",
      "sha": "ece8ed7bb81d74cb6787309fa41b7deb2e0558a3"
    }
  }
]
-- gen.sh --
#!/bin/sh
cat <<'JSON'
{"title":"Updated Title","body":"Updated body text"}
JSON
